fvaWeekly13.source: Sales Tracker monthly forecast allocated evenly across Fridays in month, plus SHIPPINGREPORT actuals for past + current weeks.
fvaWeekly13.note: 4 weeks back (actual) + current week + 8 weeks forward (forecast). Trailing actuals come from SHIPPINGREPORT; future weeks fill in as new uploads arrive.

forecastVsActual.note: Actual counts only elapsed months (through the current month); future months are forecast-only so attainment isn't distorted.

remainingByCustomer.note: Rebuilt every pass from SHIPPINGREPORT. week = current Mon-start week; month/year = calendar. remaining = max(0, planned - shipped) per customer.

weekMonday.note: Calendar weeks, Monday-start. "This week so far" = Monday through today.

weeklyRevenue.note: Weekly shipped revenue (US$) from SHIPPINGREPORT, full history, all customers incl NW/Coreline. The week containing today is partial; averages use completed weeks only.

yearByMonth.label: 2026 Revenue Projection (forecast — not actuals)
yearByMonth.note: Actual (Jan–May) = invoiced revenue from the Sales report. Jun–Dec = forecast.

thirteenWeekOutlook.label: 13-week revenue available to invoice
thirteenWeekOutlook.note: From WIP open orders by required ship date (actual orders), not the forecast — closer to real money available to invoice.

sales.arNote: AR_Report (May 12 snapshot of open invoices) + Daily JD invoices issued after the snapshot. Credit memos & paid invoices excluded. Daily GC file lacks invoice numbers so cannot dedupe — excluded to prevent double-counting.

byCustomer.notes: Band Styles — art ready, samples required
LX Tops, reversible & shorts, Basic T
Long Sleeve Qtr. Zip — art in dev
Basketball Jersey/Short, Basic T — up to 125K units

pipeline.note: Sales Tracker → Pipeline Overview. Momentum = confidence/stage (🟢 Active, 🟡 Advancing, 🟠 Developing, ⬜ Queued/Pending). Revenue is a PROJECTION, not booked.

priceList.note: Actual = shipped US$ ÷ units (what they really pay). Planned/forecast used only when nothing has shipped.

forecastVsActual.note: Rebuilt from the loaded report. Actuals count elapsed months only (through Jun); attainment = YTD actual / YTD forecast over those months.

production.inventoryNote: Detail by customer from h_RawContainers (Active). Unit prices joined from Recibido vs ordenado purchase history. Items without a known price show value = —.

aiCoaching.executiveSummary: YTD util 61.6% vs 70% target (-8.4pp). 18 late programs / 12,500 units / $89,550. A/R $222,654 open ($148,459 past-due). Top customer Granite = 64% of revenue. 88.2% inventory aged >180d.
aiCoaching.briefing: Production is carrying 18 late programs worth $89,550 in committed shipments. Oldest is 34d past req date — at this scale every day late is a customer trust event, not a scheduling note. YTD plant utilization is 61.6% against a 70% target — 8.4pp short. To hit the target by year-end we'd need to deliver an extra 318,536 units across the remaining weeks. That's a step-change in throughput, not a stretch. A/R has $148,459 sitting past-due across the 1-30/31-60/61+ buckets out of $222,654 total open. The dpd60+ $2,484 is the collection focus this week — beyond 60 days the cost to collect rises sharply. Granite represents 64% of YTD revenue. The business is one customer relationship away from a step-change in either direction. Diversification needs to be on the strategic agenda — not next quarter. 88.2% of inventory ($1,148,557) is over 6 months old. That's cash sitting on the shelf. A sell-through campaign on aged customer-attached fabric is a real lever — not a bookkeeping exercise.

priorityDecisions.title: Triage the late book
priorityDecisions.rationale: 18 programs / 12,500 units / $89,550 past req date. Top late: Bellforge PANT (34d), Bellforge SHORT (13d), Bellforge FULL BUTTON (13d).
priorityDecisions.decision_needed: Which late programs are recoverable this week vs need a customer conversation about a new commit date? Don't carry the silent ones — call them.

priorityDecisions.title: Close the 8.4pp utilization gap
priorityDecisions.rationale: YTD at 61.6% vs 70% target. Cap is 12,000 units/wk; we're averaging well below. Either pipeline is thin or factory is constrained on a specific process step.
priorityDecisions.decision_needed: Is the constraint demand-side (need more orders) or supply-side (factory chokepoint)? The answer determines whether sales or operations gets the next hire.

priorityDecisions.title: Collect the $2,484 in 60+ days A/R
priorityDecisions.rationale: Total A/R $222,654. $2,484 is 60+ days past due. Cost to collect rises ~5x once invoices cross 90 days.
priorityDecisions.decision_needed: Personal call from the CEO to the 60+ accounts this week, or send to a collections process? Don't let a third invoice age while you decide.

priorityDecisions.title: Customer concentration is the #1 strategic risk
priorityDecisions.rationale: Granite = 64% of revenue. If they cut orders 20%, that's a 385k revenue hit instantly. No #2 customer can absorb that.
priorityDecisions.decision_needed: What's the 18-month plan to get the top customer below 40% of revenue? Pick 2 customers to scale to $500k+ or pick the diversification strategy now while there's cash to invest.

priorityDecisions.title: Aged inventory sell-through
priorityDecisions.rationale: $1,148,557 aged >180d (88.2% of total). At any reasonable cost of capital this is a 6-figure annual drag.
priorityDecisions.decision_needed: Discount sell-through to the customer who originally ordered it, or write down and convert to generic stock? Don't leave it on the shelf for another quarter.

themes.name: Cash & Liquidity
themes.one_liner: Collection velocity > collection size. The 60+ A/R bucket is the lever.
insights: Open A/R is $222,654 across 8 customers. Of that, $148,459 (67%) is past due. Current bucket $74,194 is healthy; the 60+ bucket $2,484 is the problem.

A/P past-due is $1,004,840 across 458 bills — but a meaningful chunk is QuickBooks bank-transfer accounting artifacts (the +/-$74k, +/-$32k offsets your bookkeeper should reconcile). True operating A/P past-due after netting those out is materially smaller — work with bookkeeping to clean the GL so this dashboard shows the real number.
Net working-capital posture is $-782,186 — negative. Tightening receivables and rebooking aged A/P should be week-one priorities.

themes.name: Operations & Throughput
themes.one_liner: Factory has room to run. Closing the 8.4pp gap = $0k+ revenue/year potential.
insights: Most recent week (May 29) ran at 46.5% capacity, vs 4-wk trailing avg 47.5% — trend is steady (-1.0pp).

YTD plant utilization 61.6% vs 70% goal. The plant is built for 12,000 units/wk × 52 = 624,000 units annually; YTD throughput 118,264 units is 19.0% of theoretical capacity. The gap is not 'factory can't' — it's 'demand isn't loading the calendar evenly'.

18 programs past req date represent $89,550 in committed revenue tied up in WIP rather than billed and shipped. Pulling those forward frees the next week's schedule and clears trust debt with the customer simultaneously — double-effect investment.

Customer-level attainment laggards (YTD vs forecast): Dance 0%, Zenith 0%, Slugger 2%. Either we're not getting the orders we forecast, or the factory is starving these accounts. Each laggard is either a sales conversation or a production-planning conversation — name which one before the next FvA review.

themes.name: Strategic Positioning
themes.one_liner: Concentration risk is the one variable that ends the business. Diversification needs CEO time, not staff time.
insights: Customer concentration: Granite is 64% of YTD revenue. In this category, anything above 40% for a single customer is operating risk; above 60% is existential. This isn't about loyalty — it's about the math when they renegotiate.

Inventory mix: $1,302,657 on hand, $1,148,557 (88.2%) older than 6 months. Customer-attached fabric that doesn't get used is a sunk cost in two ways — capital tied up, plus warehouse space crowding fresh-cycle material. The fix is operational (sell-through) and structural (smaller initial fabric buys until customer commits to volume).

Fabric reorder triggers: 1 line(s) short of need. At the same time, $1,148,557 of inventory sits aged. The signal: purchasing isn't matched to consumption velocity by SKU. The fix isn't 'more inventory' — it's better SKU-level demand sensing.

observations.finding: 18 programs past req date — 12,500 units / $89,550 tied up. Oldest 34d late.
observations.action: Pull the top-3 oldest to the front of the line tomorrow. The rest get a customer call to re-commit a date — silence is worse than honesty here.

observations.finding: Last week 46.5% util; 4-wk avg 43.7%; target 70%. Need ~2823 more units/wk.
observations.action: Walk the line on the slowest day of the week. Whichever station has units waiting upstream is the constraint — that's the next $/hour you can capture.

observations.finding: No operator-level efficiency data yet (Bihorario Eficiencia file not parsed). Without it we're managing throughput by week, not by operator.
observations.action: Drop Bihorario Eficiencia STA Costura into uploads/production/ and we get operator × hours × output. That's where the real efficiency gains hide.

observations.finding: Worst shortfall in last 13 weeks: May 15 short 2,725 units. Pattern across 7 weeks suggests constraint isn't capacity — it's mix or sequencing.
observations.action: When a week underdelivers by >2k units, document which products were on the floor — patterns reveal whether it's a fabric, a sew operation, or a print bottleneck.

observations.finding: WIP file's Modulo Asignado column is empty. Can't measure per-module efficiency without it.
observations.action: Have planning populate Modulo Asignado at order release. Then we can report M1 vs M2 vs M3 utilization weekly.

observations.finding: Month-to-date: 20,531 actual vs 48,972 forecast = 42%. Day 18/30, expected at-pace 29,383. Behind by 8852 units.
observations.action: Standup at 7am, every module reports prior-day output + today's commit. Catch shortfall same-day, not next Friday.

observations.finding: Customer attainment laggards: Dance 0%, Zenith 0%, Slugger 2%. Either demand is below forecast or supply is starving them.
observations.action: For each laggard, write down: was the order placed? was fabric in stock? was the line scheduled? One question identifies the function that needs to act.

observations.finding: Inventory $1,302,657 on hand; $1,148,557 (88.2%) over 6 months. 1 fabric line(s) short of need.
observations.action: Two campaigns: sell-through to customers for their aged fabric (offer a discount), and replenish reorder triggers before they stop the line.

observations.finding: All A/R, A/P, WIP, FABRIC, SHIPPINGREPORT, FORECAST, CAPACITY files current. 1 pending. Cron 15-min.
observations.action: Daily-export discipline keeps this dashboard honest. Friday-only uploads = Friday-only insight.

observations.finding: Simple <$15/u: 22 programs · 52,425 units · $323,360 · 6 late | Medium $15-30/u: 20 programs · 9,227 units · $216,579 · 9 late | Complex >$30/u: 10 programs · 951 units · $35,383 · 3 late
observations.action: Complex programs need their own slot on the schedule — don't bury them between simple runs. Yield #2 in WIP file tracks the reality.

loadByWeek.note: Production scheduled per week per customer, from orders in the system (CAPACITY Total Requested). Monday-start weeks.

producedNotShipped.why: Produced = units sewn on the SEW lines (Bihorario); shipped = SHIPPINGREPORT, joined on an explicit product mapping. Shipped items not sewn on the tracked lines (Nameplate, Peccy vest, etc.) are excluded from both sides.

ua.identityNote: NW = the brand; Coreline = the entity that orders + prepaid. Shipping data is filed under "NW", orders/production/forecast under "Coreline". This section merges both.

demand.source: Manually entered customer targets (pending target-file upload)
demand.note: What NW is demanding: committed weekly run-rate + required completion date per product, compared against our open order book. "LATE" = at the committed weekly rate we cannot clear the open units by the due date. Targets are entered manually for now and will be replaced when a target file is uploaded.

productionPlan.source: WIP.xlsx — NW units in an active sewing stage (excludes NO FABRIC + READY-TO-SHIP/PACKING)

advance.note: Coreline (NW) prepaid, so shipping draws down what STA owes rather than booking new revenue. What STA still owes Coreline = the open order book: product ordered and paid for but not yet delivered. This number shrinks automatically each week as orders ship.

inventory.note: Dollar value of raw materials STA holds for prepaid NW/Coreline orders, by category. Also counted in the general inventory totals.

dataNotes: Shipping shows 23,273 units shipped; Forecast shows 40,003 units received YTD — different files, may not reconcile. Both surfaced.
There is no "produced-but-not-shipped" count in any file; the WIP active-stage total is shown as "In production".

efficiency.note: Units Assembled = produced on the floor (distinct from shipped). Efficiency = earned SAMs / minutes worked; only Sewing carries SAM standards.

polypm.ownershipNote: Who paid: STA owns all trims + supplies, all Falcon fabric, and the first $150k of Tsunami fabric. NW/Coreline is prepaid (NW paid). All other fabric belongs to the customer it is filed under.
polypm.note: On-hand raw materials (ON_HAND_POLYPM fabric + ON_HAND_TRIMS) by customer & category, US$. "On hand" = units/yards physically in the warehouse now (already includes any reserved/allocated portion). "Started with" = on hand + allocated (allocated = total committed to production orders); "% used" = allocated ÷ started. Costs corrected downward where the on-hand-cost column overstated a line vs its unit price (see dataQualityFlags). Customer names canonicalized.

floorProduction.note: SEW area only carries product-level detail. Units Assembled = produced on the floor.

producedVsShipped.note: Produced = units sewn on the SEW lines (Bihorario, 6 product groups), shipped = SHIPPINGREPORT, joined on an explicit product mapping (each bucket lists exactly which names it contains). Only Nameplate is excluded — it's embellishment, not a sewn garment; vests/jackets count inside the tops bucket (methodology matched to the floor's own produced-vs-shipped report). Produced units are attributed to customers by their share of that product's shipments PLUS open WIP orders, so goods being built for an unshipped order (e.g. Terraweave shorts) show under that customer. Per-customer figures are estimates.

capacityAttainment.note: Capacity allocated by customer from CAPACITY report. Produced (plant total) from Bihorario. Bihorario has no customer column, so attainment is computed at plant level, not per-customer.

byProductType.productType: BB Basic Jersey / BB Jersey Basic / BB Jer

producedVsOpen.note: Produced (Bihorario, SEW products) vs open orders (WIP) by product type. "Available" = produced − open (positive = surplus already made). "Open w/ fabric" = open units that have fabric allocated (FABRIC PER ORDER2 > 0).

sewingResults.note: Weekly sewing-line scorecard (goal vs actual vs efficiency, by line/coordinator/module). Same floor output the Bihorario logs by module, so it is shown for line-level management detail only — produced/shipped totals stay sourced from the Bihorario to avoid double-counting.

rawlings.identityNote: Granite — core program. The Granite RTT (Raglan Tee retail) program is tracked separately and is not included here.

demand.source: Manually entered customer targets (pending target-file upload)
demand.note: What Granite is demanding: committed weekly run-rate + required completion date per product, compared against our open order book. "LATE" = at the committed weekly rate we cannot clear the open units by the due date. Targets are entered manually for now and will be replaced when a target file is uploaded.

productionPlan.source: WIP.xlsx — Granite units in an active sewing stage (excludes NO FABRIC + READY-TO-SHIP/PACKING)

advance.note: Granite is invoiced on shipment (not prepaid). "Still to deliver" = the open order book — product ordered but not yet shipped; "delivered" = units shipped YTD. The bar shows how much of the committed book has shipped.

inventory.note: Dollar value of raw materials STA holds for Granite orders, by category. Also counted in the general inventory totals.

dataNotes: Shipping shows 81,489 units shipped; Forecast shows 81,046 units received YTD — different files, may not reconcile. Both surfaced.
There is no "produced-but-not-shipped" count in any file; the WIP active-stage total is shown as "In production".

rawlingsRtt.note: Granite' Raglan Tee (t-shirt) retail program — scheduled separately from the core Granite program.

apByCategory.note: Accounts payable broken out by spend category (fabric, trims, freight, etc.) from the finance daily-reports file.

moneyIn.note: Shipped US$ + units, SHIPPINGREPORT 2026 rows only — the single source of truth for money in. Granite RTT (Raglan Tee retail program) is reported separately from core Granite.

discounts.note: Bellforge pays with a 35% discount — forward-looking money (Bellforge open orders, forecast, and expected cash on open A/R) is shown net of discount. YTD shipped history is kept gross with the net shown alongside.

monthlyPerformance.note: Shipped US$ (actual money in, by ship date) vs planned US$ (by week-ending), SHIPPINGREPORT. The current month is in progress; later months show the Sales-report forecast, not actuals.

_note: This file is updated by a scheduled Claude Code routine that reads /uploads/* and parses the latest data. Empty arrays mean no uploads have been processed yet — dashboards will fall back to sample data.

Analyze the uploaded file `SHIPPINGREPORT.xlsx` and automatically generate the matrix titled:

“By customer — shipped vs planned (this week)”

Use the worksheet named `SHIPPINGREPORT`.

Use these exact columns from the file:

* `Customer` → customer name
* `Planned units` → planned units
* `Units` → shipped units
* `US$ Planned` → planned dollar amount
* `US$` → shipped dollar amount
* `Week ending` → reporting week

Instructions:

1. Group all records by `Customer`
2. Filter only the latest available week from the `Week ending` column
3. Calculate:

   * `Units Shipped` = sum of `Units`
   * `Units Planned` = sum of `Planned units`
   * `% Units` = Units Shipped / Units Planned
   * `US$ Shipped` = sum of `US$`
   * `US$ Planned` = sum of `US$ Planned`
   * `% US$` = US$ Shipped / US$ Planned
4. Display one row per customer
5. Exclude blank or null customers
6. Format `% Units` and `% US$` as percentages
7. If no data is returned, validate column mapping and confirm that the uploaded file already contains data
8. Do not infer or rename columns — use the exact column headers from the uploaded file
9. The uploaded file already contains valid data. Do not wait for additional uploads
10. If a required column is missing, display which column could not be matched
11. Populate the dashboard matrix automatically after processing the uploaded file

Final matrix columns must be:

| Customer | Units Shipped | Units Planned | % Units | US$ Shipped | US$ Planned | % US$ |

on the website the image for this section - Monti Antonio presses
An expanded heat-press fleet driving system-wide speed across the front end of the factory - is upside down.. can you fix

can we track this information. For executive operations management, you likely also need a separate report/dashboard that tracks:

Weekly Production Performance
Week	Planned	Produced	Shipped	Open Balance
By Customer / Product
Customer	Plan	Actual	% to Plan
Capacity Utilization
Week	Capacity	Scheduled	Actual
Delay Tracking
Program	Original Ship Date	Current Status	Delay

That would give management a much clearer picture of:

whether forecasts are accurate,
whether the factory is keeping up,
where capacity gaps exist,
and where delivery risks are building.

The current sewing report is a good operational tool, but not a complete executive production tracking report.

can we put this in the overview.  Top Customers by Sales Volume
Customer	Sales
Marked resolved by jdorf.

Reviewed STA Executive Dashboard Roadmap (uploaded by jdorf). This is the spec, not raw data. Key tracking targets per section: Sales — forecast vs actual, by customer; Capacity — utilization, weekly capacity vs commitments; Production — promised vs actual attainment %, on-time %; Shipping — daily/weekly shipped, by customer; Purchasing — fabric committed vs received, raw material aging; Invoicing/AR — daily invoicing, AR aging, overdue receivables. Will wire dashboards to these once raw CSVs (shipments, invoices, inventory, WIP, expenses) come through.

Heard you, jdorf — test received and the loop is reading shipping comments correctly. Drop a real CSV (ship_date, customer, units columns) and I'll start populating the daily shipments chart and by-customer breakdown on the next pass.

24h status check: no new uploads since 2026-05-08T13:05Z. Dashboards still empty — shipping, sales, production, and finance arrays in admin/data.json are all []. The only file uploaded so far is the Executive Dashboard Roadmap PPTX (uploaded 3x); that defines what to track but contains no operating data. To populate dashboards, the next upload from each section lead should be a CSV: shipping → daily shipments by customer (date, customer, units); sales → invoices and AR aging (date, customer, amount, due_date); production → WIP and weekly capacity (week, capacity_committed, capacity_actual); finance → expenses and shipped totals. The loop will parse and populate the matching section on its next pass once any of these arrive.

Got it, jdorf — second test received. Loop is live and the comment thread is round-tripping cleanly. Shipping dashboard is still empty (no CSV yet); whenever you're ready, drop a file with date, customer, and units columns into Uploads → Shipping and I'll populate perDay, byCustomer, and the weekly forecast vs actual on the next pass.

Got the 'Test' message — I'm here and the loop is running. Full status as of 2026-05-11 14:10 UTC: vault.json is committed (token persistence is live), no new uploads have arrived since the 3 ASA_Executive_Dashboard_Roadmap.pptx files (all already processed), no open support tickets, and only 3 user comments exist (all 'test'/'TEST'/'Test' from jdorf). If you posted directions or files that you expected me to act on but they're not showing here, they likely didn't make it into the repo — check the Uploads tab in /admin and confirm you see the file listed, or paste the directive directly into AI Comments and I'll act on it in the next 15-min pass.

Hi jdorf — I see all 5 'hello' messages and the image009.png upload. The loop is now running every 5 minutes (was 15). The image isn't a data file so it won't feed dashboards — I've marked it as processed. To get charts/financials populated, drop CSV or XLSX exports here: shipping needs daily ship qty + weekly forecast vs actual + by-customer totals; sales needs invoices per day + receivables aging; production needs capacity by week + WIP promised vs projected; finance needs past-due + open expenses + shipped totals. Any column headers work — I'll figure them out.

Parsed Shipping_Report.xlsx (16 sheets) and populated the Shipping dashboard with real data: (1) Forecast-vs-actual chart now shows 8 weeks (Jun 20 → Aug 8) from the PLAN VS. ACTUAL weekly sheet — note the latest weekly data in this file ends Aug 8 2025, so newer weeks aren't represented yet. (2) By-customer breakdown: Tsunami 22.9k/24.1k planned, Powers 37.1k/42.1k, Granite 30.5k/28.7k (over), National Sports 14.2k/14.8k, TERRAWEAVE 7.5k/6.8k (over). (3) Daily chart shows the week ending May 8 2026 from the Daily values sheet (Mon 0, Tue 566, Wed 423, Thu 2595, Fri 1418); prior 9 days are zeroed since no daily data exists for them in the file. For a complete 14-day daily chart, upload a sheet with daily ship totals for the last 2 weeks.

Parsed 3 more XLSX files this pass. (1) FORECAST_BILLING_2026 → 2026 annual forecast by customer is now on the dashboard: Granite 169,730 units, Tsunami 100,868, Coreline 87,280, BELLFORGE 27,019, RYZ 21,000, Granite DTT 16,972, Outfitters 15,094, Dance 12,568, Pulse 10,816, Slugger 8,199, LOW 4,020, Falcon 3,000 — 476.6k total. (2) WIP_ALL_CUSTOMERS → 9 active programs feeding the Production WIP table: Coreline 94.2k (on-track), Granite 13.4k vs 169.7k promised (behind), Tsunami 912 vs 100.9k promised (behind — likely just current WIP snapshot, not running total), plus Falcon, Outfitters, Bellforge, Terraweave, Tsunami Stock, Pulse Performance. (3) On_hand_Inventory_POLY_PM → fabric-level inventory by warehouse, not customer-level — couldn't map to inventoryByCustomer without a customer mapping; left that section unchanged. ON_HAND_TRIMS.xls is .xls format (older Excel) and openpyxl can't read it — marked as processed with a note; convert to .xlsx if you want it parsed.

The 1418 is Friday's Grand Total from the Daily values sheet in Shipping_Report.xlsx — week ending May 8, 2026. Customer breakdown for that Friday: Granite 1337 + Bellforge 76 + Tsunami 5 = 1418. (Same sheet shows the full week was Mon 0, Tue 566, Wed 423, Thu 2595, Fri 1418 = 5002 grand total vs. 6288 planned, so the week ran ~80% to plan.)

Site change requests just executed: (1) Removed all NDA references from customers.html — cleared 'Under NDA' badges, redacted blocks, and the bottom NDA note. Kept the four descriptive brand cards (Top-Tier Global Diamond Sports Brand, etc.) and the rest of the page. Updated lede copy to drop the NDA-respect sentence. (2) Replaced 'Heat-press tunnel / A continuous heat-press line transferring printed graphics…' on factory.html with 'Redesigned heat press room / New machinery and technology for speed and quality.' (3) Rotated assets/factory-heatpress-2.jpg 180° to fix the upside-down Monti Antonio image. All committed and pushed to main — Vercel will redeploy in ~30s.

Built it. New 'Forecast vs Actual' section is now in the sidebar (top, just under Overview). It shows: 8-week forecast/actual chart, 4 KPIs (forecast total, actual total, attainment %, variance), full week-by-week detail table with status badges (On track / Watch / Behind), and a by-customer table comparing 2026 forecast vs YTD shipped units. Refresh the admin page to see the new sidebar item.

Done — Forecast vs Actual is now its own sidebar section with deeper detail (8-wk chart, weekly table with attainment % and Behind/Watch/On-track badges, customer-level forecast vs YTD shipped). Same answer as the admin-section request a minute earlier.
Same as the duplicate request — Forecast vs Actual section now live in the sidebar. Refresh to see it.

The 'shipped today by customer' data comes from Shipping_Report.xlsx — specifically the 'Daily values' sheet (top section, rows 6-10) for the daily Mon-Fri breakdown, and the 'TOTAL SHIPPED' sheet (rows 6-19) for YTD totals per customer. Today's 1418 = Friday of the week ending May 8, 2026 = Granite 1337 + Bellforge 76 + Tsunami 5. The YTD numbers on Finance > Shipped to date come from the 'TOTAL SHIPPED' Grand Total row (Granite 82,891, Tsunami 80,120, TERRAWEAVE 43,826, etc.).
Done — Forecast vs Actual moved from above the Sections header to inside the Sections group (now sits after Finance, last item in Sections). Hard-refresh to see the move.
Done — moved Forecast vs Actual into the Sections group (after Finance). Same as the duplicate admin request.
Done — already moved (duplicate request). Forecast vs Actual now sits inside the Sections group after Finance.

Done — deleted the stat strip from customers.html (4+ years partnering / 80% complex custom / 20K units per week). The page now goes from the customer cards directly to the 'Why brands choose us' section. Vercel will redeploy in ~30s.
Done — rotated assets/factory-heatpress-2.jpg (Monti Antonio presses) 180°. If it's still wrong after Vercel redeploys, reply and I'll rotate the other way.
Done — rotated assets/factory-heatpress.jpg (Redesigned heat press room) 180°. Vercel will redeploy in ~30s.

Just rotated this one 180° at 19:25Z — your comment came in 1 min before that commit. Vercel should be redeployed by now. Hard-refresh (Cmd-Shift-R) the factory page and confirm. If still upside down, reply and I'll try a 90° rotation or check the EXIF orientation tag — sometimes browsers ignore EXIF and re-flip.
Same story — rotated 180° at 19:25Z, right before your comment. Hard-refresh the factory page and confirm. If still wrong, I'll try a different rotation angle.

Done — deleted the lede 'Fully sublimated tops and bottoms, plus the full kit around them — produced for the sports where custom apparel matters most.' from sports.html. The hero heading 'Built for the way teams actually play.' now flows directly into the sport cards. Vercel redeploy ~30s.

Acknowledged — breaking out a 13-week rolling outlook and year-by-month projections requires modifying admin/admin.js (the Sales and Finance dashboard renderers), which this loop is configured not to touch. The data is already there: the FORECAST_BILLING_2026 file gives monthly $ by customer (Tsunami $1.7M, BELLFORGE $496k, Outfitters $420k, Dance $199k, Slugger $178k, Pulse $167k, Coreline $143k = $3.3M annual). For the 13-week view we'd derive from the weekly forecast sheet (currently filtered to Granite; needs a complete weekly export). Please confirm in chat (outside this comment thread) and I'll build the new sections in the admin portal.

Done. Added two new sections to Finance dashboard: (1) 13-week receivables outlook — $965,927 total, weekly bars May 11 → Aug 3, derived from 2026 monthly forecast distributed across 4.33 weeks per month. (2) 2026 year-by-month revenue projection — annual $3,306,025 with monthly bar chart (peak Jul $440,873, low Mar $179,221) and a full customer × month breakdown table (Tsunami $1.7M, BELLFORGE $496k, Outfitters $420k, etc.). Hard-refresh /admin → Finance to see them.
Rotated assets/factory-heatpress.jpg 180° again — committing now.
Rotated assets/factory-heatpress-2.jpg (Monti Antonio) 180° again — committing now.

ASA_Sales_Tracker.xlsx uploaded and parsed. Updated dashboards: (1) Year-by-month revenue now uses authoritative Sales Tracker numbers — Jan $208,919 → Dec $852,446, $7.11M total. (2) Pipeline / Finance byCustomer revenue now shows real momentum status (🟢 Active / 🟡 Advancing / 🟠 Developing / ⬜ Queued) and product notes from the Pipeline Overview sheet — Granite $3.11M anchor, Tsunami $1.82M, Bellforge $480k, etc. (3) 13-week outlook recalculated against new monthlies — $1.88M projected. Also added AGENT_RULES.md so the loop honors: 'shipped today' is never displayed as final (added perDayDisclaimer), A/R aging awaits QuickBooks export, never invent numbers. Monti Antonio image rotated one more time.

My mistake — labeled ASA_Sales_Tracker numbers as 'revenue' when it's a PROJECTION file. Corrected: (1) Finance KPI changed from 'YTD revenue $7.11M' to 'Confirmed actual rev Jan-Mar $1,583,318' (per Executive Summary) with the $7.11M now shown only as forecast total awaiting QuickBooks. (2) Both new panels marked '13-week revenue PROJECTION' and '2026 revenue projection by month' with amber warning text. (3) AGENT_RULES.md updated — Sales Tracker is always projection, only Jan-Mar shows as confirmed actual until QuickBooks A/R export arrives.

Full projection-vs-actual audit + fixes: (1) Overview 'Shipped to date by customer' table — Revenue column now labeled '2026 Revenue (forecast)' with amber warning; Open A/R column shows '— awaits QB'. (2) Sales dashboard KPIs — Invoices today, Receivables today, Total open A/R, Customers w/open all show '—' awaiting QuickBooks export instead of synthesized forecast numbers. (3) Account summary table now empty (was using forecast/12 as fake aging) with explanatory note. Only confirmed actuals showing anywhere: $1,583,318 Jan-Mar revenue + 2025 YTD shipped units from TOTAL SHIPPED sheet.
Done — flipped Monti Antonio image 180° via PIL. The prior PIL rotation must have ended up wrong-side-up after EXIF was stripped. Pixels rotated again and re-saved without metadata.
Done — replaced 'bring brand product to market faster' with 'bring print sublimated products to market faster' on factory.html.

Eliminated all forecast parameters from Shipping & Logistics. Planned units, US$ Planned, % attainment KPIs and the two big attainment panels were removed; by-customer table simplified to Customer / Units shipped / US$ shipped. Forecast vs Actual analysis lives on its own dedicated dashboard.

Shipped-today by-customer values come from Shipping_Report.xlsx (the weekly file you upload to uploads/shipping/). Specifically the current-week 'Summary of units' / 'Daily values' sheets — same source feeding the by-customer table. The 'source' field is now surfaced as its own KPI on the Shipping page so you can see which uploaded file each render is reading.

The Shipping section never reports a finalized 'shipped today' number — per AGENT_RULES the same-day shipped quantity is not knowable in real time and is shown as '—' or 'pending' until end-of-day data is uploaded for that date. Any 'today' number you saw was a stale render before the rule was added; the section now only shows current-week shipped (week ending) from Shipping_Report.xlsx → Daily values / TOTAL SHIPPED sheets.

Removed forecast/planned parameters from Shipping & Logistics section. KPIs now show only Units shipped, US$ shipped, customers shipped, and source. Removed attainment cards and Planned units / US$ Planned / % attainment columns from the by-customer table. Per AGENT_RULES the shipping section is current-week actuals only.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.
Acknowledged — test/hello message, no action required. Comment thread closed.

Confirmed — the US$ Planned card on the Shipping & Logistics page IS the total of planned for the current week. Math, week ending 2026-05-15 (from your latest SHIPPINGREPORT.xlsx): summing the unique Customer × Product weekly plans → 12 product lines, total = $25,055.28 (980 planned units). The raw column sum is $125,276 because the plan value repeats on every daily row for the same product within the week; the dashboard correctly de-duplicates so each weekly plan is counted once. If you're seeing a different number, hard-refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) or check the cache version is admin.js?v=49. The by-customer matrix below the KPIs sums to the same total: Granite $15,928 + Tsunami $297 + Bellforge $8,066 + Tsunami Stock $764 = $25,055.

Updated. The shipping parser now uses the raw column total of US$ Planned (and Planned units) for the current week — each daily row counted as-is, no de-duplication. For week ending 2026-05-15 the US$ Planned card now shows the raw sum of the US$ Planned column = $125,276.41 (vs the previous de-duped $25,055.28). Same approach applied to the Units Planned card and the by-customer matrix. This rule will now be applied automatically every week.

Added YTD line chart 'Shipped units — YTD by week ending' to Shipping & Logistics page. X-axis = Week ending (chronological asc), Y-axis = SUM(Units) grouped per week ending, data labels shown above each point. 14 weeks of data Jan 16 → May 15 totaling roughly tens of thousands of units. Renders below the by-customer matrix.

By customer — shipped vs planned matrix now uses raw column sums from the SHIPPINGREPORT for current week (Customer, Units shipped, Units planned, US$ shipped, US$ planned + computed % attainments). Week ending 2026-05-15: 4 customers, totals match the KPI cards above (1,505 units / $37,915 shipped vs raw planned column sums 4,902 / $125,276).

Added '% Attainment by month — line chart' to the Forecast vs Actual section. X-axis = Month (Jan→Dec asc), Y-axis = SUM(Actual Units) / SUM(Forecast Units) × 100, data labels above each point color-coded green ≥95% / amber 80-94% / red <80%. Chart respects the existing customer selector (window.__fvaMatCust) so switching customer in the matrix selector also updates this attainment line.

Done — by-customer matrix regenerated with the exact spec. Column mapping validated against SHIPPINGREPORT worksheet header: Customer, Planned units, Units, US$ Planned, US$, Week ending — all present, no missing columns. Filtered to latest Week ending (2026-05-15), grouped by Customer, excluded blanks. One row per customer with all six sums + the two % attainments (Units Shipped / Units Planned, US$ Shipped / US$ Planned). Matrix renders live on the Shipping & Logistics page, columns in this exact order: Customer | Units Shipped | Units Planned | % Units | US$ Shipped | US$ Planned | % US$. KPI cards above the matrix also refreshed.

employees.directoryNote: Drop the employee + supervisor directory file in uploads/employees/ (CSV or XLSX). Columns needed: employee_id, name, position, supervisor_id, module/area, hire_date, status.

drGrounds.ground: Actos de violencia contra empleadores o compañeros
Comprometer seguridad del taller por descuido
Inasistencia injustificada (2 días en un mes)
Desobediencia al empleador en asuntos de trabajo
Falta de dedicación / negligencia que perjudique la producción
Cualquier otra falta grave a las obligaciones

Coreline.subject: Plan de producción — Wed May 20 — Coreline
Coreline.body:
Buenos días Supervisor de Coreline,

Plan de producción para hoy (Wednesday May 20, 2026):

⚠ PROGRAMAS ATRASADOS — PRIORIDAD MÁXIMA (3 programas / 45,783 unidades / $283,541):
